content.claim: A shortage of special-education teachers (gyógypedagógus) exists with the largest concentration of unfilled positions in Budapest (45 advertised positions) and unequal regional distribution, while applications to the state-subsidized special-education degree program rose 15% from 2025 to 2026 cohorts (from 3,270 to 3,768 first-choice applicants).
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Whether increased applications will address regional or functional specialization gaps is not established
Current unfilled positions by region are a snapshot; turnover and actual deployment are not detailed
No data on whether training pipeline growth will meet identified shortage within a specified timeframe
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
